RaceName: Melissa, Arabella And Oriana Hawthorne Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins in the second half of 2025, first time when beating Perfidia at Beverley (8.4f) and second time here (1m); one to note given he seems to be building up to a bigger effort this time around.

RaceName: Melissa, Arabella And Oriana Hawthorne Handicap
Spotlight: Two good runs from two for this yard, particularly when finding just a very good yardstick and a next-time-out winner too strong over C&D first time; in the mix under Daniel Tudhope, especially if first-time cheekpieces give him a lift.

RaceName: Melissa, Arabella And Oriana Hawthorne Handicap
Spotlight: Landed hat-trick last August and hit the target at Pontefract two runs ago (all 1m2f); follow-up effort at Ripon, where she's won three times, was bitterly disappointing 13 days ago and her favoured hold-up tactics aren't easy to execute around here.

RaceName: Melissa, Arabella And Oriana Hawthorne Handicap
Spotlight: Form of her yard is a positive but she hasn't shown much of late and a revival is more likely to come on AW.

RaceName: Melissa, Arabella And Oriana Hawthorne Handicap
Spotlight: Showed his tactical versatility when scoring over the extended mile at Hamilton in May (held up) and Nottingham on Thursday (made all); is unpenalised for latest success given it came in an apprentice race and Jake Dickson keeps the ride; obvious claims.

RaceName: Melissa, Arabella And Oriana Hawthorne Handicap
Spotlight: Likes it here but just one run of note since returning from a third bout of wind surgery in April and returning to this longer trip isn't a positive.

RaceName: Melissa, Arabella And Oriana Hawthorne Handicap
OwnerName: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde
Spotlight: Yet to master the start; it was seventh-time lucky over 1m here this month and quickly bounced back from a soft-ground disappointment to be third under same conditions as win last week; extra furlong here gives him more time to catch up; not ruled out.

RaceName: Dragon Symbol Standing At Whitsbury Manor British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Newcomer by Cracksman out of 11.6f/1m4f winner (RPR 88) who hitherto hasn't made much of a splash as a broodmare; others preferred.

RaceName: Dragon Symbol Standing At Whitsbury Manor British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Shaped with promise in finishing 2l third of five at Ffos Las (7.4f, good to soft) where he was the sole newcomer in the field and well-backed favourite; the form pick and evidently thought capable of better to boot.

RaceName: Dragon Symbol Standing At Whitsbury Manor British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Dropped away and trailed home last of six in 6f event at Newmarket, having started the rank outsider; again best watched.

RaceName: Dragon Symbol Standing At Whitsbury Manor British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Debutant who is the son of winners for his connections (sire 5f-7f, dam 1m2f); the betting may offer further guidance.

RaceName: Dragon Symbol Standing At Whitsbury Manor British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Achieved a meagre RPR in finishing 17l fourth of six at Lingfield (7f, good) but seemed to need the run; bigger effort may be on the way.

RaceName: Dragon Symbol Standing At Whitsbury Manor British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 70,000gns yearling; by Kameko; dam 1m2f scorer (RPR 81), half-sister to Irish Derby winner Latrobe; represents major yard; one of the likelier types among the newcomers.

RaceName: Dragon Symbol Standing At Whitsbury Manor British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Advertise half-brother to 5f scorer Dothan (RPR 81); dam maiden (63), closely related to 5f Group 3 winner Dutch Masterpiece; market informative.

RaceName: Dragon Symbol Standing At Whitsbury Manor British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Juddmonte colt, by Frankel out of 7f-9.5f winner (including 2yo/US turf Grade 1; RPR 109), from family of Dancing Brave; respected debutant, especially if the market speaks favourably.

RaceName: Dunbrody Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Smart five-time winner over hurdles and ran well in two useful handicaps in May; 3-1, jumped well out in front before tiring on chase debut over 2m4f at Kilbeggan; could be hard to beat over this trip.

RaceName: Dunbrody Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Won a point and bumper and made all to beat the useful Frankie John on hurdling debut at the Listowel festival; not seen after until meeting defeat for the first time at the Punchestown festival, too keen after his absence and dropping right out; chase debut.

RaceName: Dunbrody Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Jack Kennedy's first ride back after a layoff; dual winning hurdler and fair level of form over fences, although still not the most fluent at times; remote third behind two useful sorts in 2m novice at Roscommon last time; looks beatable.

RaceName: Dunbrody Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Half-brother to top-class State Man made a winning hurdles debut at Tramore last year; ran a decent race behind stablemate El Fabiolo at Leopardstown in March; fading in third when falling at the last at Cork and poor jumping took its toll when pulled up at Punchestown; goes chasing now; has twice been scratched on good-to-yielding ground, which is a concern.

RaceName: Dunbrody Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Useful juvenile hurdler the season before last; best run last term when fifth off 130 in 2m4f handicap at Leopardstown over Christmas; tailed off over 3m1f on chase debut, but he's definitely not a two-miler either; headgear.

RaceName: Dunbrody Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Half-brother to Ben Pauling's smart 3m chaser Henry's Friend; two wins over hurdles last year at up to 2m6f; made mistakes in his point-to-points before that and this trip will be too sharp for him on chase debut.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 50-1 and little short-term promise on recent debut at Ayr (6f, soft); different conditions today but looks best watched.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 0-6 but he has run with great credit in selling company on his last two starts; conditions will suit and he's a solid candidate once again.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Well beaten in 6f good-ground novices at Redcar and York; drop to 5f needs to have a big impact.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 4,000gns foal, 23,000euros yearling; first foal of a maiden half-sister to winners Clearpoint and Music History; first 2yo runner of the year for the stable; market instructive.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: N Bradley Racing 29, D Scott & P Spencer
Spotlight: Lacked the pace to land a telling blow when fourth of nine in a 6f novice at Redcar (good ground) ten days ago; open to improvement and one to note for market confidence.

RaceName: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 15,000euros foal, 8,000gns yearling, 18,000gns breeze-up 2yo; fourth foal; half-sister to US 6f turf winner Bright Eye Bungle; dam unplaced 7f/1m (RPR 45), half-sister to useful winners Titus Shadow (5f/6f Group 3), Orpen Shadow (6f 2yo Group 3) and Choisir Shadow (6f Listed); starts out at a realistic level and she is worth a market check.

RaceName: Whitsbury Manor Stud Supporting Inspire Foundation Handicap
Spotlight: Formerly useful; hasn't won since 2021 and ended last season with a pair of duck eggs; best watched on reappearance, unless the market suggests otherwise.

RaceName: Whitsbury Manor Stud Supporting Inspire Foundation Handicap
Spotlight: Recent form includes a turf success but he's only 1-19 in this sphere and still has a much better strike-rate on AW; below par at Brighton last time.

RaceName: Whitsbury Manor Stud Supporting Inspire Foundation Handicap
Spotlight: Form dipped last time but that was out of character; consistent sort otherwise and ran respectably, when bidding for a Bath hat-trick, the time before; may rebound.

RaceName: Whitsbury Manor Stud Supporting Inspire Foundation Handicap
Spotlight: Below par at Sandown this month but a return to his May form, namely an AW success flanked by second-place finishes on turf, would put him in the picture.

RaceName: Whitsbury Manor Stud Supporting Inspire Foundation Handicap
Spotlight: 6f winner in Ireland last term; holds weak claims on his two efforts for new stable; opposed unless the betting suggests a resurgence is on the cards.

RaceName: Whitsbury Manor Stud Supporting Inspire Foundation Handicap
Spotlight: Seven-time winner at Chepstow and finished a solid second, splitting subsequent winners, there last time; has form over this C&D and warrants respect up just 1lb.

RaceName: Coast Cremations Cup Classified Stakes
OwnerName: P And Mrs L Rowe And John Turner
Spotlight: Triple AW winner (5f/6f), but 0-9 on turf and has beaten just one rival in two starts since returning for this yard last month; look elsewhere.

RaceName: Coast Cremations Cup Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Just 1-51 and his best efforts have come on the AW; hard to make a case for even at this level; stable also runs Big Time Rascal.

RaceName: Coast Cremations Cup Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Sole win in 24 starts came in a 0-55 handicap over C&D (good) in May last year; beaten just under 2l into fourth of nine behind Washington Heir here (5f, good) three weeks ago, but may be better over this trip these days; a player despite his wide draw.

RaceName: Coast Cremations Cup Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Winner of six races and ran well when third of nine in a 0-55 handicap here (5f, good to firm) last month, but current losing run now up to 29; drawn widest and others are more compelling.

RaceName: Coast Cremations Cup Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Finished behind a couple of these over 5f here (good) three weeks ago, a race he had won the previous year; more needed.

RaceName: Coast Cremations Cup Classified Stakes
OwnerName: J May, R L Field, W Trenchard, J Pook
Spotlight: Sprung a 50-1 surprise in a 0-60 handicap at Lingfield (5f, AW) in January, but subsequent efforts suggest it may have been a flash in the pan; hard to recommend despite the drop in class; stable also runs Lesley Buckley.

RaceName: Coast Cremations Cup Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Just 2-29, but comes into this off the back of a win in a similar event at Bath (5.7f, good); not the most reliable but a player if in the same form; stable also runs Washington Heir.

RaceName: Coast Cremations Cup Classified Stakes
Spotlight: In the frame in four of his last six starts and drops to this level for the first time, but a record of 0-25 raises doubts even in this company; visor given a go this time.

RaceName: Coast Cremations Cup Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Made the most of the drop in grade when making it seventh-time lucky in a similar contest here (5f, good) three weeks ago, with three of these behind; the form is modest but he is still open to a bit more improvement; respected.

RaceName: Coast Cremations Cup Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 0-10; placed in a couple of 7f AW handicaps at the start of the year, but has regressed since then including a modest effort in one go on turf; needs the blinkers to make a difference.

RaceName: Coast Cremations Cup Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 0-7 and may not have achieved much when third of four in a 0-55 handicap at Lingfield (5f, good; first-time blinkers) 12 days ago; more needed, even down at this level; no headgear today.

RaceName: Coast Cremations Cup Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Won at Chelmsford (6f, AW) in December and ran well in his first three starts after returning to turf in April, but not so good over C&D three weeks ago; needs to bounce back, though the drop in grade is a positive.

RaceName: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase
Spotlight: One of two runners for these connections; best run when winning a valuable 2m1f Leopardstown handicap chase in 2025; hasn't been as good since, with best runs coming back at that track; questions after pulled-up over C&D last time.

RaceName: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won twice over hurdles at Clonmel last year up to 2m6f; sent chasing after and some decent efforts in defeat including over 2m4f at Kilbeggan last time; previously ran well enough over C&D; cheekpieces added; looks stable first-string so respected.

RaceName: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase
OwnerName: P Haughey & P M Haughey & John Ball
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; bumper and hurdle winner got off the mark over fences over C&D last July; disappointing at Tramore next time; respectable run in a Flat maiden on recent return; should go well.

RaceName: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase
OwnerName: Paul M Butler & James Patrick Byrne
Spotlight: Two of his three hurdle wins have come at this track; solid in defeat on his last three chase runs, including over C&D; has the credentials to run well now entering handicaps.

RaceName: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won a Leopardstown maiden hurdle and Roscommon beginners' chase last year; below that level in three chase starts since, so has to be at his best after an absence; has run well over hurdles here; tongue-tie added.

RaceName: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Bumper and hurdles winner for Willie Mullins in 2023-24; didn't add to her tally for Paul Kiely last year but came back to form on debut for this yard, winning a 2m Tramore handicap chase, beating Presenting Lad; up 6lb but can be a player with a repeat.

RaceName: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Third runner for yard; maiden hurdle winner has been well beaten in three chase runs including at this track; however, her chase mark is 8lb below hurdles one, so can't be dismissed if back to her best.

RaceName: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Two of his three wins have come at Tramore, including his sole chase score; solid in defeat at Tramore and Punchestown the last twice; ran well enough over C&D once, so could go well.

RaceName: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Dual hurdle winner for Tony Mullins last year over a similar trip to this on good ground; pulled up on stable/chase debut at Tramore behind Femme Magnifique but a solid second at Roscommon since; should run well raised 2lb.

RaceName: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Dual chase winner but hasn't won since taking a Kilbeggan handicap hurdle in 2024; was second to Femme Magnifique at Tramore and the ground was maybe too soft at Roscommon since; could rebound and be a player.

RaceName: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Second runner for these connections; course hurdle winner over 2m4f last year, was hit and miss in that sphere after; both chase runs have been here with best run a second over C&D last time; up 2lb and in grade but can be a player.

RaceName: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase
Spotlight: One of his three hurdle wins came at this track in 2024; dual chase winner afterwards; mostly below form over fences last season, but this ground suits better so could put up an improved show.

RaceName: Tintern Trails Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Improved on his three previous chase runs when winning a Limerick handicap last time over 2m1f; raised 7lb and in grade so will have to do more, but a capable 5lb claimer helps.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: A good run of form last summer included a close second of 18 in a valuable 1m2f handicap at Glorious Goodwood (good; unraced on slower); however, he's failed to live up to that form since gelded and first-time cheekpieces (discarded now) may have been more of a hindrance than a help last time; capable but risky.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win in 1m Ffos Las maiden as 3yo; beaten favourite in last four handicaps but soft and heavy ground no help in 2025 and he's performed with credit against rivals with potential this season, over 1m2f at Newbury and 1m at Sandown (both on good; probably effective on soft); solid chance at this intermediate trip for yard with 34% strike-rate here, 10-29.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: Front-runs; major improvement for this yard in 2025, winning five times, including over C&D; improved on those efforts when coming clear in fine style over 1m here (good) on reappearance but she ran poorly when going unpenalised at Redcar next time and now has an 8lb higher mark on her plate; probably wouldn't want much rain.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: Stepped up on sole 2yo run when winning Pontefract maiden (1m, good to soft) then made a spirited late dash at Hamilton (about 1m, good) to challenge one who later finished close up in the Britannia at Royal Ascot; looks sure to improve with this extra distance and likely good pace on handicap debut.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: Successful debut over 7f at Ayr (good to soft) last July; asked some stiff questions since and although he ran some fair races in defeat, his reappearance in a conditions race on Kempton AW wasn't one of them; gelded since; may fare better now handicapping but this is competitive.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: Can lead; 2-2 on this course in 2025, over 1m (good to soft) and 1m1f (good) but he hasn't been at full strength this year, including when back over C&D in April; this is a drop in grade from Newmarket in May but a complete revival is up in the air; yard runs two.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now Handicap
Spotlight: Started career with 7f wins in maiden and novice events (soft/good to soft) but things went haywire after, way behind in Group 3 (9-1) and on handicap debut at Newmarket (1m; 7-1); gelded since though and returned with a much better effort, losing out only late over C&D (good; first-time cheekpieces); back up 2lb; should find something similar if the mindset holds.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now Handicap
OwnerName: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXV & E Burke
Spotlight: Three wins at 7f and 1m (on this track; good to soft) but topped those efforts when a running-on close third, just behind Pandemonium over C&D (good; same terms) this month; should have more options now he's shown he stays this far; solid chance for last year's winning yard.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now Handicap
OwnerName: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde
Spotlight: Six wins in 2025 over a range of trips (7f-1m2f) and ground; encouraging reappearance over C&D but not so good since and doubtful whether today will be the day from out of the weights.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Overcame greenness to collar Harry Knows in the final strides in C&D contest on soft ground; 4lb worse off with that rival and concedes weight all round but may be up to the task, with progress very plausible.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Twice-raced colt who is open to further improvement; scored comfortably at Chester (6f, good to soft) to build on his Chepstow effort; one of the main form contenders.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Thames Boys, J Henwood & J Clay
Spotlight: Threatening to win a race, having finished a head second in two of his three starts, gelded prior to getting nabbed in the final strides by Flying Pirate over C&D latest; gets a 4lb pull with that opponent; leading player at the weights.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: May be sharper with Newbury outing (6.5f) under his belt but needs a big step forward, having ended up last of nine in that race.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Only eighth of nine in 6f AW event at Wolverhampton, having been one of the outsiders; perhaps one for nurseries later on.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has shown promise over 6f in both starts, catching the eye at Nottingham then seeming to run well on the figures (albeit was never better than midfield) in the Coventry at Royal Ascot; interesting back down in class.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 35,000euros yearling; Space Traveller half-brother to four winners, notably Dazzling Des (5f-6f including 2yo/Italy; RPR 85); dam 5f 2yo scorer (91); one of two newcomers in the field; market instructive.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Looked green on his way to finishing seventh of 11 in C&D event; recorded a modest RPR so needs to take a big step forward.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 6,500euros foal, 19,000gns yearling; by Gregorian and first foal of unraced half-sister to 7f 2yo Listed winner Aqlaam Vision; this debutant is a stablemate of C&D scorer Flying Pirate; market informative.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Avoids a penalty for seller win at Chester (7f, good to soft) but still needs further improvement to follow up in this stronger field and possibly isn't crying out for the drop back in distance.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Finished third of five at Bath (5.7f) last time, improving on debut effort but registering another meagre RPR; opposed.

RaceName: Sheephaven Bay And Tipsytipsters Remembering Boony Handicap
Spotlight: Sole success came at Chester (7f, good; first-time hood) just under a year ago but has finished runner-up in four of her seven starts since, the latest a narrow defeat over C&D (good) three weeks ago (third has won since); major player again from 1lb higher.

RaceName: Sheephaven Bay And Tipsytipsters Remembering Boony Handicap
Spotlight: 0-10; placed three times for Amanda Perrett, but heavy defeats in both starts since returning for this yard last month leave him with enough to prove; first-time blinkers replace hood.

RaceName: Sheephaven Bay And Tipsytipsters Remembering Boony Handicap
Spotlight: Has a consistent record here (32142132224) and has shown enough since returning on the AW in April to suggest he remains in form; should go well.

RaceName: Sheephaven Bay And Tipsytipsters Remembering Boony Handicap
Spotlight: Four wins here (three over this trip) and has dropped 2lb below his last winning mark; defied a five-month absence as a 3yo, but his reappearance efforts in the meantime suggest this first start in seven months may be needed.

RaceName: Sheephaven Bay And Tipsytipsters Remembering Boony Handicap
Spotlight: Ran well when third of 12 on his reappearance over C&D this month (Rogue Dynasty second), but all six wins have come over 7f including when recording his fourth course success back here eight days ago, for which he has a 4lb penalty.

RaceName: Sheephaven Bay And Tipsytipsters Remembering Boony Handicap
Spotlight: Having his 15th start of the year already; still 5lb above his last winning mark and although he has won over 1m on the AW, 7f may suit him better.

RaceName: Sheephaven Bay And Tipsytipsters Remembering Boony Handicap
OwnerName: P&h Moorhead, J Collins And S Piper
Spotlight: Seen more often on the AW (3-21) than on turf (1-7); back off his last winning mark, but needs to improve on his last couple of efforts.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won over hurdles at Listowel last time; previously ran well enough on debut over fences here, both over this trip; in form so has to be strongly considered.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Maiden in all spheres after 46 starts; has run well in defeat at times over fences; poor over hurdles on return but would have a squeak on his best chase runs of last season.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Maiden over fences; generally regressive over hurdles since a 2023 maiden hurdle win; broke blood vessels on return at Killarney last time and has to bounce back.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Triple point winner has shown some ability over hurdles but not so good over fences, pulled-up in a Down Royal hunter chase in March; tongue-tie on.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Dual hurdle winner won a Tramore beginners' chase winner in 2024 but is yet to win a handicap; has to improve his recent form.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Point winner showed a hint of ability over hurdles; has run well enough on both chase starts; of some interest on handicap debut down in trip.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; hurdle winner made a decent chase debut last year but not as good after; well beaten on return but is capable of better.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Good runs in two of last three hurdle starts, all at Downpatrick, winning and runner-up over 2m2f; has run well over fences before and 8lb lower mark in this sphere; looks solid.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Maiden and most of her best form has come at Tramore, running well on chase debut there last October; should come on from seasonal debut effort and has run well here too; considered.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Maiden was third in a Limerick handicap and might have found the ground too soft at Roscommon last time; dropped 1lb and mightn't be too far away if rebounding.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Shock Listowel maiden hurdle winner in 2024; hit and miss since, plus was well beaten on chase debut last time at Tramore, but can do better.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Maiden in all spheres after 21 starts; however, has been running well enough lately; second on chase debut at Limerick and two decent hurdle runs since; chance.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Two hurdle wins here last year over a similar trip to this; maiden over fences but some fair efforts; poor at Roscommon last time but can do better back here.

RaceName: Duncannon Beach Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Regressive over hurdles lately and well beaten sole chase start, so is not high on the list.

RaceName: Max Recycle Handicap
Spotlight: Won a weak 6f event at Newcastle on his handicap debut in February; not so good in either run since, especially when beaten a long way at Ripon 12 days ago on his return to turf; only of interest if backed.

RaceName: Max Recycle Handicap
Spotlight: Has won over 7f but this year's two victories have been achieved at sprint trips; not beaten far having forced the pace at Catterick (7f, good) last week; apprentice takes off a useful 5lb today.

RaceName: Max Recycle Handicap
Spotlight: 12-race maiden who hasn't offered a great deal for his current stable, including when a remote last of eight at Ayr (7f, soft) on his seasonal return 11 days ago; others much safer.

RaceName: Max Recycle Handicap
Spotlight: Both his wins have come on AW but he has had few chances on the grass and seems effective on good or quicker, as when third of eight at Catterick (7f) last week; in the mix with a repeat.

RaceName: Max Recycle Handicap
Spotlight: Won four times last year; promising third at Ayr last month but she hasn't built on it in two subsequent outings; sneaking down the weights but others appeal more for win purposes.

RaceName: Max Recycle Handicap
Spotlight: 11-time winner, including C&D, but he hasn't been at his best in two turf runs this summer; returns to 7f in a modest event and a return to form could be imminent.

RaceName: Max Recycle Handicap
Spotlight: Her first two runs (debut came when trained by Ed Walker) were respectable but she took a backwards step at Catterick (7f, good) in April; she has undergone wind surgery prior to this handicap debut; can do better.

RaceName: Max Recycle Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice as a 2yo, including a C&D nursery (soft) off a 7lb higher mark; only one effort of note this year and hopes rest on the new cheekpieces sparking a revival.

RaceName: Max Recycle Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner who got back on track with third of eight in 7f classified event at Ayr ten days ago; not discounted back in handicap company.

RaceName: Havana Grey Standing At Whitsbury Manor Handicap
Spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo whose sound form this term includes a respectable effort behind the subsequent Wokingham winner; one of his three wins was gained over C&D; solid chance.

RaceName: Havana Grey Standing At Whitsbury Manor Handicap
Spotlight: Narrowly beat a subsequent scorer (pair clear) at Yarmouth last time, taking his record over 6f to 3-6; may be capable of further progress kept to this distance; respected.

RaceName: Havana Grey Standing At Whitsbury Manor Handicap
Spotlight: Registered his success in a 2yo sales race over 7f; seemed unsuited by the drop back to 6f last time (handicap debut) but this stiffer track may help; 2lb lower.

RaceName: Havana Grey Standing At Whitsbury Manor Handicap
Spotlight: Still seeking first win on turf but has posted two encouraging efforts this term, going close in first-time cheekpieces at Goodwood latest; possibilities in the retained headgear.

RaceName: Havana Grey Standing At Whitsbury Manor Handicap
Spotlight: Highly tried last term after novice success; never figured in Windsor handicap on reappearance but may fare better with the outing under his belt; check the betting.

RaceName: Havana Grey Standing At Whitsbury Manor Handicap
OwnerName: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner
Spotlight: Has performed creditably a few times this term but her form surprisingly dipped at Windsor last time, has gained both wins at that venue; others preferred.

RaceName: Havana Grey Standing At Whitsbury Manor Handicap
Spotlight: Campaigned mostly in lower grades and has done her winning on Wolverhampton AW; doesn't look the answer in this scenario.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Dual AW winner early in the year, latterly off a 3lb higher mark, who's gone off the boil; this is less competitive on just his second start in 0-60 company but he's still to show he's as effective on turf (two runs to date).

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Exposed maiden who hasn't seen it out in two runs over further since switched to turf; the drop in trip should help and he's been eased 5lb but he's hard to recommend nonetheless.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Yard had a few easy winners of late, including a couple at big prices; this filly, who threatened to pick up a small handicap when the hood first went on, has had excuses the past twice and is one to consider.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Bumped into one on his handicap debut early in the year before showing the right attitude to win off 1lb lower next time (both Polytrack); didn't look to see out 1m2f on soft ground back from a break recently and holds obvious claims at this lower level.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Bumped into an improver when second at Lingfield (turf) last month but she's twice run below market expectations in 0-55 company since returned to turf; essentially a regressive maiden.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won a Ballinrobe maiden hurdle and a Galway handicap over 2m last year; some good chase runs in defeat since, including here; can improve from return effort; major step up in trip, but a player if staying.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Point and bumper winner didn't win in five hurdle starts, but off the mark over fences in a 2m5f Leopardstown handicap in March; twice runner-up since including over 3m; softer ground might not have suited at Roscommon last time, and this should suit better with cheekpieces retained.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: A winner over hurdles and fences at Punchestown up to 3m; some good runs in defeat in the spring before a lesser Ballinrobe effort; unlucky to unseat early last time; can be a player.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
OwnerName: Desmond O'Brien & John T O'Neill & J P Flavin
Spotlight: Flat winner in UK and has won twice over hurdles at 2m4f for this yard; won on chasing debut at this track over 2m4f in 2024 but fell on only chase run since; poor on Flat and hurdles last two runs, but not dismissed.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
OwnerName: Paul M Butler & James Patrick Byrne
Spotlight: Won consecutive races over fences last year including here over 3m1f; best run since was back here, so could put two lesser runs behind him on return to this venue.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Point and maiden hurdle winner; was disappointing in first-time blinkers at Listowel last time when keen; cheekpieces tried today; ran well enough on sole chase start last year; not ruled out.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Bumper winner in UK and both hurdle wins for this yard have come at this track up to 3m; regressive over hurdles last autumn and well beaten in two beginners' chases; has to return to form.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles when winning a 3m handicap at this track last time; chasing debut today but did run well on sole point start, so has to be considered.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; hurdle and chase winner has lost his form since last autumn, though was hampered when pulled-up last time; needs a revival.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won over hurdles at this meeting last year and added a chase win at Tramore last time over 2m6f; the runner-up then has won since, so should be capable of going well off a 5lb higher mark.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Some ability in points but not much in six hurdle starts and will have to do better now tackling fences.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Maiden in all spheres after 32 starts but was second at Downpatrick last time over a similar trip; raised 1lb but is up in grade today, so needs a bit more.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap
Spotlight: He had dropped a long way in the weights prior to joining Tim Easterby and he has been competitive in some useful events this year, including a third of 19 behind a smart prospect at York (7f, good; did best of the prominent racers); down in grade today and he should go well.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap
Spotlight: C&D win off this mark in April; mixed since but his latest third at Ayr (6f, soft) was a sound effort and today he has the assistance of an apprentice who is good value for his 7lb claim; high on the list.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing Lxxiv & M Hulin
Spotlight: Returned from wind surgery with a comfortable success at Pontefract (6f, good) in April; creditable runs in defeat since but she will need to pull out more to defy this mark.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Still seeking his first handicap success and he has been beaten as favourite in both starts this year; on a dangerous mark and likely to be popular again.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Arrives in the form of his life, completing a turf hat-trick at Ayr (7f, soft) 11 days ago; that made it six wins from his last nine starts and he is respected despite the 6lb rise and stronger opposition.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap
Spotlight: 1m maiden win at Gowran Park in April 2024 seems a distant memory; out of sorts on Flat and over hurdles from July 2024 onwards; starts out for new stable after 399 days off with a lot to prove.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with a 7f AW win at Newcastle; not in much form since returning from a three-month break and too much to prove for now.

RaceName: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Has run well over C&D in the past but Hamilton is her spiritual home and she has added another two wins at that venue this summer, the latest a comfortable front-running success (6f, good to firm) in Class 6 company five days ago; 5lb higher over an extra furlong on a different track and in a higher grade so this may not be her day.

RaceName: Madar Corporation EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 5yo by Eastern Anthem; first foal; dam unplaced 1m/1m2f in France, half-sister to smart 2m/2m3f hurdle winner Sempre Medici; probably best watched on belated debut, unless the market suggests otherwise.

RaceName: Madar Corporation EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Holds weak claims on the form he has shown in two bumpers and a Flat contest.

RaceName: Madar Corporation EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Eye-catching fourth at Kempton (AW) in late 2024, then was absent until sixth of 14 at Windsor (1m2f) this month; remains open to improvement, with the reappearance effort under his belt.

RaceName: Madar Corporation EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Failed to live up to expectations (4-5 favourite, finished only eighth of nine) in 8.5f AW maiden at Wolverhampton but is evidently thought capable of much better; big step forward is not out of the question.

RaceName: Madar Corporation EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who is proving consistent; good second in both turf attempts, latest at Windsor (1m, good to soft) behind a useful sort; leading form contender upped further in distance.

RaceName: Madar Corporation EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Twice-raced gelding who gives the firm impression that handicaps will be more suitable shortly.

RaceName: Madar Corporation EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Study Of Man half-sister to four winners, notably Ebury (1m; RPR 100); dam 1m4f scorer (82), closely related to Derby runner-up Dragon Dancer; represents esteemed connections; interesting newcomer.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: Took advantage of a drop in the weights when winning a 0-55 contest by a neck over C&D three weeks ago; 5lb rise puts him on his previous winning mark and although not the easiest of rides, Tom Queally knows him well (2-8 on him); major player.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: Front-runner who has won three times at Bath (1m/1m2f) since the start of last month, but he didn't get home when fourth of nine over C&D (good to firm) nine days ago; may not face too much competition up front so could still be dangerous.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: At the veteran stage and has finished unplaced in his last ten starts, but now 8lb below his last winning mark; could still have a big effort in him.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins came over C&D in the spring of last year and well treated on his best AW form of last winter; recent efforts leave him with a bit to prove.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: Having dropped a long way in the weights she looked about ready to strike when third of eight at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) last month, but she ran poorly back there (1m4f, good) 17 days ago; enough to prove after that.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: Just 1lb higher than when winning at Nottingham (1m2f, good) last July but he needs to improve plenty on his last few efforts.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: 0-17 but in the frame nine times, the latest when second of 11 at Chepstow (1m2f, good to soft) 15 days ago; no reason why she shouldn't go well again.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to fire this year and has a bit to find with Bohemian Breeze on their meeting here three weeks ago, but he is 12lb lower than when completing a hat-trick around this time last year (first two legs came over C&D); worth a second look.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Mares Beginners Chase
OwnerName: J P O'Flaherty & Mark E Phelan
Spotlight: Listed bumper winner and disappointing that she hasn't managed to win in eight starts over hurdles; dam was a smart chaser, she loves good ground and has O'Keeffe's assistance, so not without positives for chase debut.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Mares Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Useful hurdler with placed form in Listed and Graded company as a novice; much improved on fourth chase start at Tramore when close second, around 2l in front of Pampar Lady in race in which Future Prospect departed two out; not sure to appreciate this extra half-mile.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Mares Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Three wins in handicap hurdles, including repeat victory in Clonmel contest over this trip last month; some smart chasers further back in her pedigree, but she's entitled to improve for this first run over fences.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Mares Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Point winner and best of these over hurdles; ran creditably against some of last season's leading novice mares in two chase spins in midwinter; off since finishing down the field in the Martin Pipe, but goes well fresh and at her local track.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Mares Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Point winner was useful in bumpers and over hurdles; can be keen and was so when without hood for chase debut at Tramore; 4-9 fav then, raced prominently and jumped well in the main, but went left at a few fences and looked vulnerable when taking a heavy fall two out; not crying out for this extra half-mile, but reapplied hood should help.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Mares Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Back to form over hurdles in recent months, but she's a long way inferior to several of these in that sphere and it's late in the day to be going chasing; best watched.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Mares Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Rattled up a hat-trick over hurdles last summer, at up to 2m3f; after seven months off, ran well on chase debut at Tramore, upsides Future Prospect when that one departed two out, then just got tired and finished behind Beauforts Storm; jumping was a bit novicey then, so should improve for the experience and the run.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Mares Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Point and maiden hurdle winner; jumped poorly in pair of midwinter chase outings against geldings; should come on from respectable hurdles run last month and now tried in blinkers.

RaceName: Catherine & Charlotte Roche Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins from four Flat starts in 2026 include a C&D success last week (had Sophiesticate two necks back in third); likely has more to offer at this kind of trip and could easily shrug off a 4lb penalty; yard took this last year.

RaceName: Catherine & Charlotte Roche Handicap
Spotlight: Last three wins came over C&D; hit flat spot but rallied for third under these conditions last week; obvious claims of turning the tables on winner Gemini Man given she was beaten two necks and has a 4lb pull; 7lb claimer Kyle McHugh enlisted too.

RaceName: Catherine & Charlotte Roche Handicap
Spotlight: Landed a gamble on British/stable debut in 1m5f Ayr classified contest 11 days ago (soft), leading on the bridle 2f out before driven clear to win by 4l; today's underfoot conditions might be quicker, and this is a sharper track, but she could well be up to defying a 7lb rise.

RaceName: Catherine & Charlotte Roche Handicap
Spotlight: Unplaced all eight starts and finished weakly when tackling this kind of trip once before.

RaceName: Catherine & Charlotte Roche Handicap
Spotlight: Record of one win from 23 starts; close second at Hamilton this month but was quickly left behind by the winner when filling the same position there on Thursday; passed over.

RaceName: Catherine & Charlotte Roche Handicap
Spotlight: Winner under both codes in 2024 but hasn't convinced when going beyond 1m1f on the Flat previously.

RaceName: Showcasing Standing At Whitsbury Manor Bibury Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Thrice-raced filly and Irish Oaks entry whose form includes a promising effort behind a useful stablemate on debut; very easily justified favouritism at Ripon (1m3f, good) most recently; likely to build on that win and show further improvement; respected.

RaceName: Showcasing Standing At Whitsbury Manor Bibury Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has a record of 2-3 in handicaps, the wins over 1m2f at Nottingham (good to soft) as a 2yo and York (good) in latest outing; likely to be suited by this new trip and is the proven handicapper in the field; possibly hasn't reached his ceiling yet.

RaceName: Showcasing Standing At Whitsbury Manor Bibury Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has shown steady progress in five maiden races, as form figures of 321 this season reflect; form of half-length win at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) was franked when the runner-up went one better next time; goes handicapping with a solid chance.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
Spotlight: Completed a hat-trick since the end of last month, including two all-the-way successes over C&D; doesn't have to lead and his 4lb penalty for his win eight days ago (three of these behind) shouldn't preclude another big performance.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins over 7f/1m here; runner-up in his last two starts back at this venue including when beaten half a length by Dion Baker eight days ago; a 4lb pull plus Jack Callan's 3lb claim gives him every chance of reversing the form.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
Spotlight: Four wins over 6f/7f here; just 2lb above his last winning mark but well held in both starts since returning last month, including when behind three of these over C&D eight days ago; more needed; stable also runs Jackson Street.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
Spotlight: Dual course winner (5f/6f) who remains just 1lb above her last winning mark; 0-11 over this trip and further, though, so others seem more likely.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
Spotlight: Usually seen on the AW and just 2lb higher than when winning at Lingfield (7f) in December; placed just once in 11 starts on turf, though, so not hard to have reservations.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
OwnerName: J Harley, T Jacobs, G Moore
Spotlight: Third in his last two starts over 1m/1m2f here, but now 0-19 despite a slipping mark and not sure dropping back to this trip is the key; blinkers return.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
Spotlight: Just 1-26 but has run well in both starts since joining this yard, including when a close third behind Dion Baker and Havana Mojito over C&D eight days ago (flashed tail); shouldn't be far away again.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
Spotlight: Won three times over 1m here last summer and 5lb lower than for the most recent; hasn't threatened in her last five starts, though, and another furlong would be preferable.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
Spotlight: Well beaten in his last two starts including on his turf debut here (6f, good to firm) last month; probably best watched until returning to 0-50 classified company.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
Spotlight: Hasn't threatened in seven starts and needs the hood to make a big difference.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
OwnerName: C Seeney, S Holden, Mildean , M Robinson
Spotlight: Out of the frame in all ten starts and impossible to make a case for.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Won a Tipperary maiden hurdle last year, and met trouble in running when held twice afterwards; tongue-tie added to hood for chase debut; bred for this game but is stable second-string and has been off nearly a year.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Easy point winner for Donnchadh Doyle but has been disappointing under rules, including over fences last thrice, weakening each time; heading for the sales soon, one would imagine.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Long-standing maiden finally got it done when winning readily over hurdles last month; second in three of 13 chase starts, and some fair runs over fences without being placed in April/May; these types can kick on once they get their heads in front, and Donoghue prefers him to Sophia Rose.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Modest pointer and hurdler is having first run over regulation fences; sure to be a big price.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Second in a point; modest enough over hurdles, but would have needed latest run in Punchestown handicap where he ran well to a point; cheekpieces swapped for blinkers on chase debutant from decent family.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Won non-event over hurdles by a street at Kelso in May, beating only other rival to stand up; still, he just gets in here on the ceiling rating and half-brother and dam won over fences; chase debut.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Did well over hurdles last year, winning a couple of handicaps; unfancied and has shown little in three starts this year, including chase debut in a Limerick handicap.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Prominent racer over hurdles, won 2m4f handicap at Clonmel in April and fair third back there last time; chase debut.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Out of form over hurdles since winning off 107 at Galway in September; only 10s for Listowel handicap on chase debut the time before, but made no impact; best watched after a break.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Hurdles mark now 109 after Listowel handicap win over a furlong farther (good to yielding), his first start for five months; they were mulling over staying over flights a bit longer, so interesting that the brother to Scottish National winner Captain Cody is now deemed ready for chasing; ran well in sole 4yo point.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Twice second from seven starts in points; first hurdles run at Leopardstown seemed promising but hasn't built on that; chase debut.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Won a point at four; second in maiden hurdle here last year and fair handicap run when last seen in January; hooded for chase debut.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Three wins over hurdles; fair bit of experience over fences and was second of 16 in C&D handicap in April; had In The Trenches behind when third to a smart mare at Kilbeggan and latest hurdling third was fine; new rider aboard.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Fund Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Three seconds from eight starts over fences, and just run out of it close home in Killarney handicap seven weeks ago; mark went up 4lb to 106 after that; apparent second-string for Gavin Cromwell, but has her chance.

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Handicap
OwnerName: Grange Park Racing VIII & Mrs R Carr
Spotlight: 3-12 on AW compared to 0-17 on turf but last week's second at Nottingham (5f, good to firm) suggests he can pick up a win on the grass off this sort of mark; has to enter calculations, although any easing of the ground possibly not ideal.

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Handicap
OwnerName: Making Headway Racing And K Scott
Spotlight: Two 5f wins for David O'Meara at Newcastle this year, the latter achieved in smooth fashion at the beginning of this month; never a threat when fifth on stable debut at Ayr (5f, soft) ten days ago, taking turf record to 0-9; the headgear she's worn on her last ten starts is left off here.

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Made all over C&D (good) at the beginning of the month and very nearly repeated the feat last week, collared only in the final strides; same mark today and another bold bid looks assured in her current mood.

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden who has form claims on her close third at Redcar (5f, good to firm) last month but hasn't come close to reproducing it twice since, latterly with cheekpieces added (retained).

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Improved when third in 5f Catterick novice on good to firm last month but didn't come close to backing that up when beaten just under 9l into fourth on handicap debut there (good to soft) 19 days ago; early days but hard to be confident after that.

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Easily best run when all-the-way winner for Robert Cowell in 5f Wolverhampton handicap in March but well held all three runs since; changed hands for 2,200gns since last time and this yard switch needs to spark a resurgence.

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Handicap
OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: A switch to turf has seen an upturn in her fortunes, tackling 5f for the first time when opening her account at Ripon (good) 13 days ago; raised 5lb but possible she can pull out more.

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Won six times on AW in the first part of 2024 but there hasn't been much to cheer about since; 0-27 on turf but has run well over C&D and the forecast rain won't bother him; still too much to prove for comfort.

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: C&D scorer who ran his most encouraging race of the campaign when third (Wee Mary second) over C&D (good) eight days ago; his best form of 2025 came at this time of the year so it's possible he can build on that.

RaceName: Watch On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Arrives out of sorts, finishing well behind Wee Mary and stablemate Sixcor in two outings over C&D this month.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap
Spotlight: Prescott filly who has been absent since making all in Newcastle novice event (1m, AW) on final 2yo start, beating a useful rival who currently has a mark of 102; the type to progress further and do well in handicaps; very interesting off an opening mark of 75, especially if the market speaks positively.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap
Spotlight: Four-race maiden who has consistent 7f form, fourth in Sandown handicap most recently; possibilities, provided this extra furlong proves a source of progress.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap
Spotlight: Has shown most of her form over 7f, including AW maiden success as a 2yo and good second at Chepstow (soft) most recently; may be effective back up in trip, provided she settles (sometimes takes a good hold).

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden prior to winning twice at Windsor (good to firm) and running creditably at Nottingham (good) since upped to 1m; creeping up the weights but may remain competitive kept to this trip.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap
Spotlight: Beat only two rivals at Bath (1m, firm) to open his account at the ninth attempt; however, may build on that success and do better still, in keeping with his pedigree (from a family that has served his connections well); shortlisted.

RaceName: Molson Coors Handicap
Spotlight: Absent and gelded since running well at Lingfield (1m, AW; handicap debut) in January; another step forward is required but market support should be heeded.

RaceName: Yeomans Toyota Proace Handicap
Spotlight: Best effort in eight starts for Richard Hannon when beaten a neck in a Wolverhampton nursery (8.6f, AW) in December; makes his stable debut after five months off having changed hands for 6,000gns in February; market helpful.

RaceName: Yeomans Toyota Proace Handicap
Spotlight: A bit in and out in her six starts, but has shown ability as when fourth of 12 at Leicester (7f, good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) in April; now 5lb lower and would have a good chance if rediscovering that form.

RaceName: Yeomans Toyota Proace Handicap
Spotlight: Bounced back to form when third of seven on his stable debut at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) last Friday; should go well again.

RaceName: Yeomans Toyota Proace Handicap
Spotlight: Showed ability in four starts last year, but not built on it in three outings since returning last month; down another 3lb but best watched for now.

RaceName: Yeomans Toyota Proace Handicap
Spotlight: Won a nursery over C&D (good to firm; first time cheekpieces) last September and was returning from 166 days off when a clear second behind an easy winner at Chepstow (6f, good to firm) on Saturday (taking her record under Ashley Lewis to 122); return to this trip should suit; respected.

RaceName: Yeomans Toyota Proace Handicap
Spotlight: 1-12 on the AW, the win coming from 1lb lower at Wolverhampton (7f) in February; fair fourth of eight back there (first-time blinkers) last time, but this is a tricky track on which to make his turf debut.

RaceName: Racing Again July 3rd Rated Novice Chase
Spotlight: 1-10 in points and won 3m hunter chase at Cork in April; well beaten in Tramore hunter chase two weeks later; likely to struggle in this company.

RaceName: Racing Again July 3rd Rated Novice Chase
Spotlight: Won on fifth start in points and decent hurdler ran well in Punchestown festival handicap; little wrong with his chase debut second at Kilbeggan, where he was fairly nimble when in close to his fences.

RaceName: Racing Again July 3rd Rated Novice Chase
Spotlight: A 120-rated hurdler, so just sneaks in here, and won good-ground maiden over this trip last summer; after seven months off, was on his head the whole way and jumped novicey and left when third on chase debut at Tramore; going back up in trip at a left-handed track looks a smart move; related to winning chasers and likely to improve.

RaceName: Racing Again July 3rd Rated Novice Chase
Spotlight: Galileo 5yo is a dual Flat winner and landed Limerick maiden hurdle last year; would have needed first run of the year when never a factor on chase debut at Kilbeggan; jumped okay but probably best watched here.

RaceName: Racing Again July 3rd Rated Novice Chase
Spotlight: Fair good-ground hurdler over around 2m4f, easy winner at Bellewstown last summer and comes here on the back of a good run at Listowel; nicely-bred sort goes chasing now under regular rider.

RaceName: Racing Again July 3rd Rated Novice Chase
Spotlight: Fair hurdler's sole win was over 2m6f and stays 3m; second in a couple of points, but jumping lacked conviction when long way behind Chichester Park at Kilbeggan last month on first outing since August.

RaceName: Racing Again July 3rd Rated Novice Chase
Spotlight: Cracking dual-purpose 9yo has won four hurdles and gained most valuable of six Flat wins at Cork in May; lacks a bit of scope for this game, as shown when a long way off Chichester Park at Kilbeggan; better at the latter venue since, but was still low and left at a few fences.

RaceName: Racing Again July 3rd Rated Novice Chase
Spotlight: Has had six chase runs, best effort when second over 3m2f at Fairyhouse in January (jumped left); not far off the placed horses when fourth to an easy winner over 2m6f last time; has had a break and runs well fresh; place claims.

RaceName: Racing Again July 3rd Rated Novice Chase
Spotlight: Good-ground bumper and maiden hurdle winner for Willie Mullins; four chase runs for this yard have been okay; well held in a handicap last time, albeit that was a decent contest and the experience will stand to him back in a novice.

RaceName: Racing Again July 3rd Rated Novice Chase
Spotlight: Bumper and 2m4f maiden hurdle winner; never competitive against smart rivals in first two chases, but was running with some credit when exiting two out both times; a clear round the obvious priority now.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Faced a very stiff task when introduced in a six-runner Curragh event in which Aidan O'Brien and Robson De Aguiar were responsible for the first three; of some interest in this grade, but likely to need more experience.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Exors of the Late J C Fleming
Spotlight: Has finished in front of only two of his 16 rivals in two C&D appearances.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: E25,000 yearling; fifth foal; half-brother by Sergei Prokofiev to two winners; dam a low-rated maiden, half-sister to a US Grade 3 winner.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: E42,000 yearling; fourth foal; half-sister by Gleneagles to two winners; dam ran only twice, out of half-sister to Derby winner Ruler Of The World.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Never in contention on debut in a 6f maiden at Fairyhouse; big price then despite having Colin Keane in the saddle; likely to need more experience.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Good pedigree, dam won the Rockfel Stakes, out of Oaks/Irish 1,000 Guineas winner Imagine; not beaten far when fifth of eight over C&D on debut.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: E10,000 yearling; first foal; dam placed on AW (RPR 58), half-sister to 1m1f winner Searcog; apprentice aboard with a senior rider on the stable's September Duke.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Looked in need of further when staying on in a Listowel maiden over an extended 6f; beaten 14l in fifth, considerable improvement needed.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Slowly away on debut at Fairyhouse; mid-field finish was respectable in the circumstances, 2 1/2l behind sixth-placed Nando Royale.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: E7,000 yearling; second foal; half-brother to a winner in Spain; dam unraced half-sister to two winners; speedy pedigree.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Kiva Cromwell & Coragh Bloodstock Limited & Fergus Grimes
Spotlight: Noted staying on when sixth of 17 at Fairyhouse and fifth-placed horse won at Naas last week.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: E20,000 yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to a US 7f dirt winner; dam a Canadian stakes winner; good ground should suit.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Dropped away at Fairyhouse, finished behind Nando Royale and Banks Of The Boyne.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Beaten favourite over C&D on debut, beaten 2.5l into fourth of 8 behind Arizona Raider; may recover losses now having been denied by a nose at Leopardstown 12 days ago; cheekpieces go on.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Fair fourth over 1m at Leopardstown on the second of two starts at two; appeared not to see out the extended 1m1f at Gowran eight weeks ago; 72-rated filly needs to find a little extra to win a maiden.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Failed to make an impact on debut at Gowran; safely held by Elusive Echo on that running.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Down the field at Leopardstown, tailed off at the Curragh; tongue-tie tried now.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Has struggled in two Dundalk outings; safe to leave out of calculations.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: First home in a barrier trial at Naas; wore a tongue-tie (retained) on debut in a 1m Gowran maiden early this month, raced prominently behind Star Of Beauty before the pair were overtaken by the favourite Amenita; fair chance of reversing form with the more exposed runner-up.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: 6-1 on debut at Navan; failed to make much impression in a maiden won by the promising Sparan Nua; likely to be capable of better; dam, a half-sister to Derby winner Harzand, was a Group 3 winner over 7f at two.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Midfield on debut at the Curragh (6f, soft); not a bad effort when fifth of 14 at Down Royal (7f, good); needs to raise her game.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Fourth in a claimer at Ballinrobe; unplaced on handicap debut at Gowran; official rating of 64 implies she needs to improve considerably to win a maiden; checkpieces added.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: No threat on heavy at the Curragh in March; may be seen to better effect on a sound surface; cheekpieces on and champion jockey aboard.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Unplaced in two Gowran outings; held by Star Of Beauty and Elusive Echo; more likely a handicap prospect after this.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: David K Kelly & Mrs Pauline Condon
Spotlight: Rated 77; some solid efforts in defeat, including third of 13 in a Naas maiden last August (6f, good); bounced back from a disappointing handicap run at the Curragh when second in a C&D claimer; made most of the running at Gowran; not guaranteed to uphold form with the less exposed Elusive Echo.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Down the field at Naas on her only start at two; from a small Cork stable that had a runner placed at 50-1 at the Curragh last Friday.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Looked raw when finishing at the back at Gowran; likely to need more experience.

RaceName: Town And Country Cakes Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Looked to have stamina issues in bumpers; been gelded since last seen a year ago and is likely one for handicaps further down the line (rare Flat runner for his yard).

RaceName: Town And Country Cakes Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: A D G Oldrey & G C Hartigan
Spotlight: Gelded brother to the yard's decent bumper (on debut)/1m4f-1m6f winner Gnomon/half-brother to their 7f-1m winner Newbolt; trainer does well here (16-57 with 3yos) and the market will guide.

RaceName: Town And Country Cakes Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-brother to two French winners who shaped fairly behind Celestial Cen on his soft-ground debut over this trip early in the month, having got behind after a slow start; will have learned from that.

RaceName: Town And Country Cakes Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Needed her debut experience, when a big price over 7f (warm Newbury maiden), and took a good step forward upped to this trip at Goodwood last month; should have more to offer.

RaceName: Town And Country Cakes Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Twice placed over shorter at Dundalk in the spring before changing hands and gelded; edged left, away from the rail, at Windsor for his new yard a fortnight ago (first-time tongue-tie; made most; Adalo well behind); anti-clockwise course should help.

RaceName: Town And Country Cakes Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Remote last on his debut over this trip and again well held upped to 1m6f last time; likely he'll find life easier once handicapping for a yard also responsible for leading fancy Mother Dear.

RaceName: Town And Country Cakes Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Like Liberate shaped nicely coming from off a steady pace on her Lingfield (1m2f, soft) debut early in the month; started a big price that day and is entitled to improve.

RaceName: ROA/RSA Shared Ownership Day Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Useful hurdler for Ben Pauling, winning two of his ten starts; has quickly found his feet back over fences with back-to-back small-field wins, the latest with ease at Uttoxeter 17 days ago; up 6lb and may face a sterner test from Captain Cool but still the one to beat.

RaceName: ROA/RSA Shared Ownership Day Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won first two starts over fences (2m1f, good) and shaped just as well when reverting to hurdles off 13lb lower, finishing runner-up at Southwell this month; latest second, over fences at Perth 23 days ago, was perfectly respectable but his current mark looks demanding.

RaceName: ROA/RSA Shared Ownership Day Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 2-7 over hurdles for Dan Skelton before being sold for £5,000 after bleeding from the nose and pulling up on chase debut at Bangor in May; made a satisfactory start for this stable when runner-up to Tellherthename at Uttoxeter two weeks ago but is 4lb out of the weights here.

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins for George Boughey (two on AW); five outings for this yard provide little encouragement; however, now in 0-60 company for the first time (has won off 70) and may be competitive.

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran winner of 14 races (11 on AW); shaped well in his opening two turf runs this year; second over 7f at Leopardstown (yielding to soft) and fourth over C&D (good); a bit below that level at Gowran on latest.

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: Made the breakthrough in a 7f Dundalk handicap in March; followed that with two seconds (at Dundalk and on turf at Leopardstown); below his best at the Curragh and Listowel, before returning to form with a Limerick fourth.

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: Ended last season with two fair runs on turf, notably when second over 1m at Bellewstown; progress not sustained on AW; hard to fancy on her first run since January.

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: Bolted up over 7f at Listowel on seasonal debut; raised 13lb for that, well held in mid-field at Fairyhouse a fortnight later; won over C&D early last season.

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: Second in a 21-runner 1m1f contest at Leopardstown last August when trained by Dermot Weld; ran his best race so far for this stable when third over same course and trip recently.

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
OwnerName: Ms Julianne McNally & Colette McNally & D McNally
Spotlight: Fifth career win when revelling in the mud at Listowel (7f, heavy) last September; out of form in two runs this season.

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: Won a handicap at Tipperary last summer; has failed to make an impact in three outings this season, needs to show signs of revival.

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: Won a maiden and a claimer in Britain; poor form for this yard until 2l fourth of 15 at Killarney last month; backed that up with a second placing at Down Royal; strong contender.

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: Showed next to nothing in three 7f AW maidens; made turf debut over 5f at Cork; not disgraced, bearing in mind that his rider had to cope with a slipped saddle; could be a dark horse on handicap debut

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: Weak form in maidens; down the field in handicaps over C&D and at Fairyhouse; needs to improve with blinkers on.

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: No sign of winning potential in maidens last year; has struggled in two handicap attempts; tongue-tie on now

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: Two moderate runs at two; in rear over C&D a year ago on her only start at three; hooded.

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: Weak form last season; never involved in 5f races in her first two handicap starts; fine effort when placed at 66-1 in a C&D claimer; limitations underlined when she was sent back into handicap company at Down Royal.

RaceName: Jimmy Murray Agri Services Handicap
Spotlight: First time in the frame when fourth over C&D in May, further progress is feasible.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Pembrokeshire Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Pleasing debut when a clear second (Impierious third) to a useful prospect at Newmarket in April; bit below that level when some way off his two market rivals next time but is one of the likelier winners for a yard going well.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Pembrokeshire Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Hasmonean Racing, S Hope & S Barrow
Spotlight: Well-related gelding who finished a few lengths behind Dark Whisper when both made their debut at Newmarket in the spring; ran to a similar level in a small field at Doncaster early in the month and has fair claims on either effort.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Pembrokeshire Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten a long way on his debut early in the month but pulled hard over 1m2f and didn't see it out; should fare better but will need a good step forward if he's to get involved.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Pembrokeshire Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Did too much too soon on his debut and bettered that at Windsor last time, when going at it up front for a long way with the long odds-on favourite; that effort sees him enter the equation for a yard among the winners.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Pembrokeshire Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Some way off the front pair when third in a German maiden last November (1m1f, soft); 40-1, ran as the market suggested he might on his first start since being gelded a few weeks ago; handicaps are likely next on the agenda.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Pembrokeshire Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Gelded, early-foaled 28,000gns 2yo who's the first foal of Shale, a Group 1 juvenile winner in Ireland (7f); stable (also saddle Elenaya) form might be better and he'd need to be above average to take this on debut.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Pembrokeshire Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Tailed-off last in two runs, spread well apart, for the Newland/Insole yard last year; goes in a first-time tongue-tie back from six months off and likely she'll find life easier once handicapping.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Pembrokeshire Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-sister to three-time Group 1 winner (Hong Kong) Waikuku and the yard's 1m2f Royal Ascot winner (Group 3) Waipiro; easy to back and looked to need her debut experience a few weeks ago (7f, good to soft), when shaping nicely; high on the shortlist.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beat one rival in a maiden point in March 2025 but failed to complete in five subsequent runs; showed some promise when 24l third on hurdle debut at Warwick (2m, good) this month but failed to build on that when well beaten over C&D two weeks ago.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Modest maiden pointer who was beaten 35l on hurdle debut at Warwick (2m, good) this month; has plenty to find and it is easy to look elsewhere.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has failed to complete in three starts in points and finished tailed off on sole hurdle start at Hereford (2m4f, good) in March last year; hard to recommend on stable debut.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: He hinted at ability in two hurdle runs this spring following wind surgery, albeit beaten 25l and 15l, most recently when fourth of eight at Warwick (2m3f, good) in May; may be one for further down the line but has place claims.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has reached the frame in both hurdles starts but beaten 34l and 23l in the process; could be one for handicaps in due course but, in a race lacking depth, he may be capable of hitting the frame again.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed ability in early bumpers but form has gone the wrong way, including on both starts over hurdles this summer; best watched for now.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Progressed nicely in three bumper runs, winning here (good to firm) on his latest start by a wide margin; appeals as the type to make an impact in this sphere and he's respected on hurdle debut, especially now stepping up in trip.

RaceName: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 2yo Listed winner on the Flat; made a promising start over hurdles when third at Newbury (2m, good to soft) in January but was well beaten at short odds at Catterick (2m, soft) later that month; subsequently underwent wind surgery and, if able to reproduce his earlier effort, he's firmly in the mix on this comeback.

RaceName: Conor Cassidy Car Sales Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark in Naas claiming maiden last September (1m, yld-sft); solid effort a month later on next start and handicap debut at Leopardstown (1m, yld-sft); up 4lb for that 0.5l defeat; better ground and longer trip are queries here and lacks a run, but top rider booked so check market.

RaceName: Conor Cassidy Car Sales Handicap
Spotlight: Left previous modest form behind to land a 1m4f (yielding) handicap here on latest start in May; the step up in trip was offered as the reason for the improvement in form, so the drop back in distance here may not be ideal; raised 6lb and quicker ground a worry too in this deeper race.

RaceName: Conor Cassidy Car Sales Handicap
Spotlight: 0-7; best run came when a 5l second of 8 in a Killarney maiden last October (1m, sft-hvy); shaped promisingly enough when denied a clear run back there in May (1m3f, good), but needs to rebound from dire effort at Newbury last time.

RaceName: Conor Cassidy Car Sales Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent form in maidens at 1m-10.5f on the AW and on good and yielding ground; opening mark of 72 may be on the lenient side and he's interesting on handicap debut.

RaceName: Conor Cassidy Car Sales Handicap
