Spotlight: Promise in maidens at 7f-1m on soft ground; soundly beaten in first two handicaps, his latest second at Gowran (9.5f, yielding) a remote one; eased 1lb and cheekpieces go on; more needed.

RaceName: Conor Cassidy Car Sales Handicap
Spotlight: Best run in maidens a 9l fourth at Leopardstown (1m2f, yielding) in April; handicap debutante not obviously well treated, but worth checking in the market.

RaceName: Conor Cassidy Car Sales Handicap
Spotlight: Ordinary form in maidens and hasn't made an impact in first two handicaps; only midfield when stepped up to 1m2f at Down Royal latest; eased 4lb but others make more appeal.

RaceName: Conor Cassidy Car Sales Handicap
Spotlight: Didn't achieve much in four maiden starts at 7f-12.5f on variety of ground; blinkers go on for handicap debut; can only be watched.

RaceName: Conor Cassidy Car Sales Handicap
Spotlight: AW claimer winner at 7f last November; stayed on late when ninth of 14 over 7.5f (good) here on seasonal return and handicap debut last month, beaten 7.5l; not bred for this far, but worth a go at this trip on the evidence of that last run; could outrun likely odds.

RaceName: Conor Cassidy Car Sales Handicap
Spotlight: Knocking on the door, runner-up in three of last four starts; however, all three of those runs came on rain-softened ground and concerning that her three previous runs on good ground have resulted in heavy defeats.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Mayfair Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Regressed sharply last year and has been tried over various trips of late; one of his better efforts over 1m here last Tuesday but is worse off back out of handicap company and no certainty to repeat the dose.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Mayfair Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Well drawn when third at Chepstow early in the month, wasn't at the same course last time; won't mind the drop from 1m and is one to consider for 5lb claimer Harry Vigors, who's enjoying a purple patch.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Mayfair Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Not beaten far on his turf/handicap debut a fortnight ago but didn't achieve much at a big price (no real improvement on his AW work).

RaceName: West Wales Properties Mayfair Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Inconsistent filly who offered more on her second run back a fortnight ago, when finding 6f an inadequate test (sole win came over this trip on soft ground); bit to find at the weights but is capable if on a going day.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Mayfair Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Infrequent winner who was again turned over as favourite when never happy behind Prince Ali last time; wants a good test over this trip these days and others make greater appeal.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Mayfair Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Six-time AW winner who sprung a surprise under Zoe Lewis when having a rare turf start (1m, soft) early in the month (back in cheekpieces); this trip's a bare minimum and history suggests he's no good thing to back up that effort.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Mayfair Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Back in form since dropped to this level and proved 7f within range at Brighton last week, despite not looking in love with the course; her claims are obvious under Billy Loughnane (8-41 for the yard) as long as there's not too much rain.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Mayfair Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Well supported down to this level at Brighton early in the month, when unlucky not to go closer; conversely, proved easy to back at the same course last time, when slowly away and soon behind; much will hinge on how she takes to first-time headgear.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Mayfair Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Huge prices for four runs, finishing well back each time, three times after fluffing the start; little to recommend her.

RaceName: SolidCam UK Partner Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won three consecutive small-field races in the autumn, the latest a Plumpton handicap (2m4f, good), but has been well below par on his two subsequent starts, including at Market Rasen in May after a break; remains feasibly treated if the cheekpieces and tongue-tie prove a positive addition.

RaceName: SolidCam UK Partner Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Yet to add to his 2m Ludlow maiden hurdle win but has run with credit since, most recently on handicap debut at Market Rasen when staying on over 2m4f after racing wide; likely to be involved off an unchanged mark if stamina holds up over this longer trip.

RaceName: SolidCam UK Partner Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Lacks consistency but capitalised on a reduced mark when ending a near three-year drought at Ayr (extended 2m4f, soft) in April, but has failed to reproduce that effort since and the 6lb rise is not the chief concern; however, he could still go well if at his best.

RaceName: SolidCam UK Partner Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Two Class 5 wins to his name, most recently at Huntingdon (extended 2m4f, good to firm) in May, and his follow-up effort under a penalty was a respectable first try at 3m2f; drop back in trip should suit and although his revised mark has now taken effect he is still respected.

RaceName: SolidCam UK Partner Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Returned to form in the autumn, landing a deserved C&D success in October and running well in defeat on two subsequent starts up to around 3m; remains on a tough mark but should be all the sharper for this month's reappearance.

RaceName: SolidCam UK Partner Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Recorded back-to-back wins at Ffos Las (2m4f, good) in the spring and has continued in good form off higher marks since, most recently going down by a head over C&D last month; nudged up another 3lb but remains hard to ignore in current form.

RaceName: SolidCam UK Partner Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won two chases at Hereford (2m5f, good) in April and May 2025 but has been more miss than hit since, with both runs this year underwhelming; reverts to hurdles with a visor added off a fair mark but needs to turn things around.

RaceName: SolidCam UK Partner Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 2-11 over hurdles and produced his best effort in a while when runner-up at Huntingdon (extended 2m4f, good) in May last year, but was pulled up on his return from a break in February, which tempers enthusiasm; returns from another layoff with a bit too much to prove at present.

RaceName: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Won 7f Tipperary maiden on debut last year; no further success, but holds a fair chance judged on his third at Leopardstown on his penultimate start; was drawn wide when unplaced at the same venue last time.

RaceName: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Too high in the ratings since a Gowran maiden win last autumn; however, he ran well over 1m4f here on latest, form boosted when runner-up Obscenity continued a good spell of form with a Gowran success.

RaceName: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Useful juvenile hurdler for James Owen in 2024-25 season; won twice, and stayed on well for third (best of the home team) in the Fred Winter; AW winner, 0-6 on turf, placed four times; makes Irish debut after a long layoff and probably needs a bit further than this.

RaceName: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Regressive since back-to-back wins at the Curragh early in the 2024 season; no impact in three runs last year; mid-field on reappearance at Limerick; well beaten over C&D last time; first time with cheekpieces

RaceName: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Six-time Dundalk winner, including three in a row there during February/March; turf record is modest (1-18), and he is 11lb higher than when fourth at Naas on his final start last season.

RaceName: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent form last year; won at Killarney's July fixture and ended the campaign with seconds at Leopardstown and Dundalk; could make her presence felt if reappearing in peak shape

RaceName: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Five-time winner for Joe Murphy; yet to find his form for this stable; reduced rating now (4lb below his last winning mark) but needs to show some spark before becoming a betting proposition again.

RaceName: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice in France; probably best watched since Nicola Burns prefers her father's other runner, the race-fit Grey Leader.

RaceName: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Improved from seasonal/yard debut when scoring over C&D last month; well beaten at Gowran since.

RaceName: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Won three handicaps last season (one of them over C&D) and went up aggregate 18lb in the process; yet to hit form in three runs this term; now back on her last winning mark; worth considering.

RaceName: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs Helen Butler & John A Griffin & Mrs Bernadette Fahey
Spotlight: Just one win from 29 attempts; consistent when making the frame on three occasions at Dundalk last November/December; failed to reproduce that form in two races at the same venue in January.

RaceName: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Enjoyed soft ground last year, runner-up over 1m at Galway, went one better on heavy at Listowel; below best this season; first-time blinkers failed to do the trick at Gowran.

RaceName: Sean Cleary Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Showed significant improvement in first-time blinkers to win a handicap over an extended 1m at Gowran last October; proved he could handle a sound surface with a follow-up success at Dundalk in November; went up 14lb in total; further improvement feasible this term, but blinkers left off here.

RaceName: WWP 34 Years Finding Customers Their Dream Homes Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time Bath winner who has a win and a second to his name in two runs here (both 1m); adaptable regarding tactics but hasn't run over 7f since his 3yo days in Ireland and he's drawn widest back up to a career-high mark; wouldn't want much rain.

RaceName: WWP 34 Years Finding Customers Their Dream Homes Handicap
Spotlight: Best efforts come over C&D, including a surprise win off 2lb lower last August; absent for three months since an ordinary AW comeback but plenty of rain would suit and it wouldn't be a shock were he to pop up.

RaceName: WWP 34 Years Finding Customers Their Dream Homes Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins and a second to her name in three runs here, all over C&D; slid to a dangerous mark now, being 8lb lower than when last successful, but she's lost her way this year; soft ground ideal, so the more rain the better for her.

RaceName: WWP 34 Years Finding Customers Their Dream Homes Handicap
Spotlight: Won five times during the AW season; much better treated on turf now but has never been as good or consistent on grass, and he ran poorly at Yarmouth when last seen; Billy Loughnane is a positive booking but he wouldn't want too much rain.

RaceName: WWP 34 Years Finding Customers Their Dream Homes Handicap
Spotlight: Lost his way for this yard, pulling hard the past couple of times; conditions fine off a career-low mark but he has something to prove back from a short break, during which time he had a wind operation.

RaceName: WWP 34 Years Finding Customers Their Dream Homes Handicap
Spotlight: Surprise winner of a heavy-ground 0-55 handicap over C&D last autumn; didn't offer a lot on her comeback early in the month and is hard to recommend after that.

RaceName: WWP 34 Years Finding Customers Their Dream Homes Handicap
Spotlight: Well-beaten third on her turf/handicap debut last month before not seeing out 9.5f on Tapeta last time; this is a bit easier back over 7f but she's starting to look exposed.

RaceName: WWP 34 Years Finding Customers Their Dream Homes Handicap
Spotlight: Possible that soft ground didn't suit when beaten at a short price over C&D a fortnight ago; also possible it came too soon after his small-field Salisbury win but that race isn't working out; bit to prove currently.

RaceName: Davisons Law Moving You Forward Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Struggled after returning to this stable last season but has shown more encouraging signs this spring, running respectably on the Flat before a better effort in a Cartmel selling hurdle (2m1f, good) in May; faces a tougher task back in open handicap company under top weight but has place claims.

RaceName: Davisons Law Moving You Forward Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Comfortably doubled his hurdle score when winning at Hexham (2m, good) nine days ago, escapes a panalty for that success and Tom Midgley can claim his 3lb allowance here; another bold bid looks likely.

RaceName: Davisons Law Moving You Forward Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Completed back-to-back wins last spring, including over 2m here, but has been badly out of form since returning from a break in December and failed to justify market support when pulled up at Newton Abbot this month; on a good mark but hard to recommend in current form.

RaceName: Davisons Law Moving You Forward Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 3-19 over hurdles, the latest coming at Huntingdon last May off 5lb higher; no match for Tyson (down to go in the 7.18) in his repeat bid in May on return from a break, though that was still encouraging; on a workable mark if able to build on that effort.

RaceName: Davisons Law Moving You Forward Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won at Wincanton (1m7f, soft) off this mark in November but failed to build on it afterwards; has offered more in two runs since returning from a short break but needs another step forward to figure.

RaceName: Davisons Law Moving You Forward Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 46-race maiden under all codes, including 19 starts over hurdles; runner-up at Clonmel (2m1f, yielding) in April but failed to build on that next time; makes stable debut for a leading yard and, though he has plenty to prove, it'll be interesting to see what they can do with him.

RaceName: Davisons Law Moving You Forward Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 11-race maiden, though placed on three occasions; has offered little encouragement in three runs since joining this stable, including when tried in cheekpieces last time.

RaceName: Davisons Law Moving You Forward Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Five-time Flat winner (1m4f); justified strong market support when opening hurdles account here (2m, good to firm) in May, under Sean Bowen and shaping as though this longer trip would suit; up 6lb but remains capable of better after just eight starts in this sphere; leading contender.

RaceName: Davisons Law Moving You Forward Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Best effort over hurdles when third of six at Fakenham (2m, good) in April but has failed to build on that and has been well held on both handicap starts; others have more obvious claims.

RaceName: Davisons Law Moving You Forward Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Showed little in three hurdle runs last year but returned from a break with a more encouraging effort when fifth on handicap debut at Southwell (2m, good) recently; needs another step forward but has place claims.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Irish EBF Lenebane Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won this race (yielding) on first start for this stable last season and has held form well since; runner up in Irish Cesarewitch at the Curragh (2m1f, yielding) then landed the Group 3 Loughbrown Stakes there in November; close second in Limerick Listed race (12.5f, soft) on seasonal return 11 days ago; top rated and obvious chance, but could do with some rain.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Irish EBF Lenebane Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: 4-10 on turf including  a handicap win here off 74 last July (10.5f, yielding); won the Group 3 Alleged Stakes at the Curragh in April (1m2f, soft); not a bad run in 10.5f Chester Group 2 latest (10.5f, good), shaping as though ready to step back up to this sort of trip; leading claims.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Irish EBF Lenebane Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Soundly beaten on debut in a Sligo bumper (2m2f, good) in May but left that behind to score on Flat bow in a Down Royal maiden (1m5f, soft) 10 days ago when 25-1, taking the scalp of Daddy Long Legs; stiffer task here, however, and quicker ground to deal with too.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Irish EBF Lenebane Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Listed winner at Leopardstown (1m2f, heavy) last season; nowhere near that level in two runs this term, slowly away on both occasions; has form on good ground and at this trip so while she has a bit to find on the figures, she's not out of this if rediscovering her best.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Irish EBF Lenebane Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Off the mark for this yard in 1m1f Leopardstown handicap (soft) 19 days ago; all three of her wins have come in handicaps on soft/heavy and she was well held on her only previous try at this level at Gowran in May; cheekpieces go on; Shaool looks yard's best hope.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Irish EBF Lenebane Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won three handicaps in Britain for Sir Mark Prescott; very pleasing start for this stable when a 2.75l third of 9 in a Cork Group 3 (1m4f, good) behind the promising Sparan Nua; a bit to find on paper here, but will love the ground and more black type could be on the cards.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Irish EBF Lenebane Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Naas maiden (10.5f, yielding) winner for Dermot Weld last season; well held on sole previous try at Listed level and with an official rating of 82, this looks too ambitious on her seasonal return and stable debut.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Irish EBF Lenebane Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Mrs H Shanahan & Mrs Jane Shanahan
Spotlight: Built on promise shown in first two maiden starts to run away with a 1m4f Fairyhouse maiden (good) 18 days ago, her first run at this trip; this is much tougher, but she's totally unexposed at this distance and it wouldn't be a surprise were she to run well.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Carmarthenshire Handicap
Spotlight: Sharply regressive since winning a soft-ground Bath maiden (5.7f) last autumn, turning in three tame efforts since returned to turf; this is easier again but it's hard to find many positives.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Carmarthenshire Handicap
Spotlight: Expensive to follow since handicapping but valid excuses as favourite last time, when finding the leaders not coming back from a wide stall; been found a weak 0-55 handicap in first-time headgear.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Carmarthenshire Handicap
Spotlight: First signs of life on his handicap debut 12 days ago, when dropped from 6f; that was a weak, small-field affair but he's been dropped 2lb and it gives him something to build on.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Carmarthenshire Handicap
Spotlight: Inconsistent maiden who's been turned over at short prices a few times of late, the past twice in 0-50 classified company; a poor effort 12 days ago will need forgiving for a yard also responsible for Follow My Heart.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Carmarthenshire Handicap
Spotlight: Poor in three AW starts before gelded; much better when dead-heating in a weak, small-field Chepstow handicap last month but failed to build on that on very soft ground last time; goes in first-time headgear under Billy Loughnane (first ride for the yard).

RaceName: West Wales Properties Carmarthenshire Handicap
Spotlight: Found more behind a better-fancied stablemate on her handicap debut but didn't build on that last time; has a first crack at 5f under Cieren Fallon, who's currently 0-7 for the yard.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Carmarthenshire Handicap
Spotlight: No penalty for winning a weak apprentice handicap at Beverley last Tuesday but she scrambled home at a big price there and is unlikely to go up much anyway; now has to back it up and doubt she'd want rain (taken out on good to soft ground recently).

RaceName: Laura Horsfall Racing Club Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Defied a double penalty when completing her hat-trick over hurdles at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) 18 days ago; connections wasting little time in switching to fences for handicap debut and she looks to have been crying out for this longer trip; on a workable mark and could improve again.

RaceName: Laura Horsfall Racing Club Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Promising fourth on hurdle debut but well held in two subsequent starts for Martin Brassil and little impact when switched to fences on handicap/stable debut at Warwick (2m4f, good) after a break; still early days but he doesn't look particularly well treated on that evidence.

RaceName: Laura Horsfall Racing Club Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Dual chase winner but has yet to match that level in three starts this year, including when seemingly not staying 3m at Hexham 24 days ago; drop back in trip should suit but he needs to stage a revival.

RaceName: Laura Horsfall Racing Club Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Front-runner whose two wins last year came at Sedgefield when making all; has had excuses since returning from a short break in May, possibly stretching his stamina over 2m5f first time and finding the race coming too soon latest; only 1lb higher than for his latest win but needs to step up.

RaceName: Laura Horsfall Racing Club Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Maiden point winner at the 11th attempt prior to winning stable debut at Bangor (3m, good; refitted tongue-tie) four days ago; carries a penalty and drops back in trip but looks a big danger.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner for Harry Kelly last year; close third on sole Flat start at Bellewstown (1m4f, good) in April 2025; pulled up over timber when last seen last July; worth a market check on return.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: Definite promise on debut in a Punchestown bumper last month, weakening late on into an 8l fourth of 13 (RPR 97); could go well on first start in this sphere.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: Rated 74 after three maiden runs at 9.5f on soft, yielding and good to yielding ground; hasn't shaped like he's been crying out for this longer trip the last twice and might prefer easier ground.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: 25-1 and tailed off in a Kilbeggan bumper on debut in April; can only be watched on Flat debut after that effort.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: Dual-purpose; very modest form so far in both codes; fell over hurdles at Limerick when last seen in March; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: Best run came on debut in a heavy ground Naas bumper; soundly beaten in Flat maidens the last twice including over C&D; qualifies for handicaps after this.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: Placed in one of six starts over timber and rated 103 in that sphere; not disgraced on Flat debut in 1m7f Leopardstown maiden (gd-fm) on latest; more needed down in trip.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: Bumper winner and rated 105 over hurdles; not beaten far on Flat debut at Leopardstown in June 2025 (1m7f, good, RPR 80), but below that level when beaten 17.5l at Thurles in October; place shout if he repeats that Leopardstown effort.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: Best run for Willie McCreery came in a 1m2f Curragh maiden (yld-sft) last September; tailed off on AW when last seen in December; new yard now; likely best watched unless the market speaks.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: Standout effort on penultimate at Dundalk (10.5f) when a 0.5l second of 12 (RPR 85); looked in need of further that day but disappointed upped to 1m5f at Down Royal latest, although soft ground may not have suited; blinkers go on now; chance.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: Soundly beaten on debut at Dundalk in March 2024 for Dermot Weld; back from mammoth layoff with much improved effort on first start for this yard at the Curragh (1m4f, yielding) 27 days ago; should be sharper for that and can go closer now, once she doesn't 'bounce'.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: Bumper winner rated 95 after five runs over hurdles; pulled up in that sphere on latest; beaten 35l on sole Flat start at Galway last July when 66-1; others make more appeal.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: Shaped as though further would suit when a 16l seventh of 17 on debut at Gowran in April (9.5f, soft) and bred for this sort of trip too; related to good ground winners, so should handle conditions and eye-catching jockey booking; chance.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: Very modest so far over hurdles (rated 86) and in two previous runs on the Flat; seems safe to rule out.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: 0-18 on the Flat and rated just 32; would be a shock winner of a maiden.

RaceName: Ladies Day At Roscommon On Monday, 6th July, 2026 Maiden
Spotlight: No show on debut at Dundalk last November; not beaten far on turf debut at Killarney (1m, good), staying on well; runner up when stepped up to 1m4f in Limerick claimer; soundly beaten in maiden there latest, but soft ground a possible reason for that; not discounted returned to a sounder surface.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Ceredigion Handicap
Spotlight: Dual winner last year, latterly off 4lb higher, who's running in a 0-65 for the first time; finished last on his latest start but wasn't beaten far and Cieren Fallon (0-5 for the yard) is a positive booking; ought to fare better.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Ceredigion Handicap
Spotlight: Done a lot of his winning later in the year but offered more in the returning blinkers last time, back from a couple of months off; boasts a decent record when dipping below 0-70 company and won't mind any rain.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Ceredigion Handicap
Spotlight: Missed a chunk of his career and has been steadily regressive since; hit and miss last backend and is entitled to need this at his age after nine months off; wouldn't want too much rain.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Ceredigion Handicap
Spotlight: C&D win last August came off an 8lb higher mark; the booking of Billy Loughnane will draw some in, back to the visor worn for his two AW victories, but two poor efforts on turf lately will need overlooking.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Ceredigion Handicap
Spotlight: Been around a bit and has regressed sharply for this yard, mainly over hurdles; Sapphire Sirocco looks the more likkely of the stable pair.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Ceredigion Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran who's done a lot of his winning in the summer and always liked it round here; back in form over hurdles last time and is 2lb lower than when going in here last July, so he needs a second look.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Ceredigion Handicap
Spotlight: In good form for her new yard back on turf, appreciating the stiffer test when winning off 2lb lower last time; having vastly experienced 5lb claimer Ryan Kavanagh up won't hurt and she's shortlisted.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Ceredigion Handicap
Spotlight: Nothing like as good or consistent these days but back in form last time (1m4f, 0-50) to confirm his liking for this course; gives his trainer, who saddles two others, a decent hand but wouldn't want the ground too soft.

RaceName: West Wales Properties Ceredigion Handicap
Spotlight: String of poor efforts to date, latterly when a remote last behind Adrian in a 0-50 here; the new trip will need to bring about a lot of improvement.

RaceName: Sunday 12th July Is Family Day Mares' National Hunt Flat Race (Cond'/Amateurs) (Cat 2 Elim') (GBB)
Spotlight: Beaten favourite in a couple of Irish points but snapped up for £25,000 in May and made no mistake when winning bumper debut at Worcester (good) three weeks ago, scoring with a bit in hand; penalised for that success but, with Harry Atkins' claim offsetting the bulk of that, he's the one to beat.

RaceName: Sunday 12th July Is Family Day Mares' National Hunt Flat Race (Cond'/Amateurs) (Cat 2 Elim') (GBB)
Spotlight: Well beaten on this month's Newton Abbot debut (2m1f, good to soft) and has plenty to find on that form; pedigree suggests she'll be seen to better effect over further in due course.

RaceName: Sunday 12th July Is Family Day Mares' National Hunt Flat Race (Cond'/Amateurs) (Cat 2 Elim') (GBB)
Spotlight: Fifth foal; closely related to useful 2m7f hurdle winner Doc Penfro and a half-sister to 2m5f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Juniper; dam won bumper and over hurdles/fences; appeals on pedigree but is likely to need a stiffer test of stamina to be seen at her best.

RaceName: Sunday 12th July Is Family Day Mares' National Hunt Flat Race (Cond'/Amateurs) (Cat 2 Elim') (GBB)
Spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister to 2m5f chase winner Another Million; promising fifth of ten on last month's Southwell bumper debut (2m, good), shaping as though the experience would do her good; open to improvement and could make the frame.

RaceName: Sunday 12th July Is Family Day Mares' National Hunt Flat Race (Cond'/Amateurs) (Cat 2 Elim') (GBB)
Spotlight: First foal; dam 7.5f winner (RPR 80), half-sister to useful winners Regulation (1m Flat and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase) and Decorative (6f-1m); makes some appeal on pedigree and market support would heighten interest.

RaceName: Sunday 12th July Is Family Day Mares' National Hunt Flat Race (Cond'/Amateurs) (Cat 2 Elim') (GBB)
Spotlight: Half-sister to the yard's fair hurdler I'm A Starman; faced an impossible task leaving the back straight on her Worcester debut (good; tongue-tie) 19 days ago but kept on with some purpose to finish a never-dangerous fourth; likely to come on for that experience and merits respect.

RaceName: Sunday 12th July Is Family Day Mares' National Hunt Flat Race (Cond'/Amateurs) (Cat 2 Elim') (GBB)
Spotlight: Dam unraced half-sister to high-class bumper and jumps performer Outlander and smart hurdle/chase winner Western Leader; bought for 13,000euros after a runner-up finish on her third Irish point start and that experience should stand her in good stead on debut under rules; respected.
